name: AuraFlow
/*1: Description shown in app stores and install prompts.
description: Gesture-controlled ambient soundscape for meditation, focus, and sleep.
/*3: URL opened when the installed PWA is launched from the home screen.
/*4: Scope restricts which URLs are considered part of this PWA.
/*6: standalone: the app runs in its own window without browser chrome.
This is the closest to a native app feel — address bar, back button,
and tab bar are all hidden, maximising the immersive no-screen experience.
/*7: Fallback display modes in order of preference.
fullscreen would hide the status bar but is not supported everywhere.
minimal-ui is the last resort before falling back to browser tab.
/*8: Lock to portrait — landscape breaks the gesture interaction model.
/*10: background_color is shown on the splash screen before the app paints.
/*11: theme_color tints the browser UI chrome (status bar on Android,
title bar on desktop) to match the app's dark aesthetic.
/*13: Two sizes cover the full range of use cases:
192px — Android home screen and splash screen.
512px — Play Store listing, desktop install prompt, and high-DPI displays.
Both are placed in public/icons/ so Vite copies them unchanged to dist/.

icons./*: purpose 'any' means the icon is used in all contexts.
maskable allows Android to crop the icon into a circle or squircle.

/*15: App shortcuts appear on long-press of the home screen icon (Android)
or right-click of the taskbar icon (desktop).
Each entry deep-links directly into a specific scene.
/*17: Declares that the app prefers to handle its own links rather than
opening them in the browser. Required for desktop PWA link capture.
/*18: Enables launch queue so the app can respond to shortcut URL parameters.
/*20: Screenshots are shown in install prompts on Chrome and Edge.
Add real screenshots to public/screenshots/ and uncomment this block
